_doc: Each flag is matched case-insensitively against product title, description, and idea text. 'aliases' adds common spelling variants. Risk levels: high = force copyrightRisk='high', cap score at 30, surface in verdict. medium = advisory in prompt, AI weighs context. low = informational only.

flags.notes: Disney files DMCA aggressively; even abstract silhouettes have been taken down
Same enforcement as Mickey; ear silhouette alone has triggered takedowns
Lightsabers, helmets, droids all enforced; Disney acquired Lucasfilm in 2012 and ramped enforcement immediately
Character likeness enforced even without the Star Wars name in the listing
Helmet shape alone has triggered takedowns; one of the most litigated 3D print designs globally
Exact shape is trademarked; 'astromech droid' workaround is common but not safe
Extremely popular and extremely enforced; Disney acted within weeks of The Mandalorian airing
Character and helmet design enforced aggressively; fan workarounds ('space dad' etc.) do not guarantee safety
Specific helmet design protected post-2019 series; numerous Etsy shops taken down for this design
The blade-and-hilt design is trademarked even without Star Wars branding; wall mounts and displays frequently DMCA'd
Full character roster enforced; Iron Man helmet is one of the most DMCA'd 3D models on the internet
Helmet shape is registered trade dress; numerous Etsy shops suspended
Logo and suit design enforced; even the web pattern has been disputed
Norse mythology origin creates a grey area but Marvel has trademarked specific MCU Mjolnir designs; generic rounded hammers are lower risk
Tower, logo and team name all enforced; Avengers Tower Echo Dot holder is a known flagged design
Shield design is trademarked; round shield with star = instant flag
Mask and claw designs enforced
Disney inherited enforcement rights from the Fox acquisition
Character likeness enforced; baby Groot especially popular and especially flagged
Shared IP between Marvel and Sony; both have issued takedowns
Claws and character likeness enforced; long history of Etsy takedowns
Nintendo enforces all 1000+ characters; Pikachu and Snorlax are the most common 3D print DMCA targets
One of the most DMCA'd 3D models globally; shape is globally trademarked
Extremely popular as Apple Watch charger base; Nintendo has taken action on this specific design on Etsy
Popular in 3D printing community; enforced like all Pokémon characters
Popular ghost design; enforced
Nintendo is the single most aggressive IP enforcer in gaming; Mario character enforced globally
Same enforcement as Mario
Character enforced; Nintendo does not license fan merch
Character enforced; 3D print community frequently targeted
Character enforced
Character enforced
Full IP enforcement including characters, symbols, and game titles in listing text
Zelda symbol trademarked; appears frequently in 3D prints; Nintendo enforces this symbol specifically
Shield design from Zelda is trademarked; full enforcement
Deceptively friendly-looking character with full trademark enforcement
Character enforced; Nintendo has issued takedowns for DK-related 3D prints
Newer IP but fully enforced by Nintendo
Characters and Leaf symbol enforced; hugely popular 3D print subject
Samus helmet is one of the most popular 3D cosplay prints and a frequent DMCA target
Logo and character roster enforced; listing the title is itself a risk
WB aggressively enforces; Hogwarts crest, house symbols, wand designs and Deathly Hallows symbol all flagged
Name and crest enforced; 'school of witchcraft' workaround is unreliable
Symbol trademarked by WB; one of the most commonly flagged HP items on Etsy
Bat symbol is a registered trademark; one of the most enforced comic book IPs
S-shield logo is trademarked; DC enforces actively
Logo and character enforced; shield design disputed in multiple Etsy cases
Character enforced since 2018 film
Partially based on public domain archetypes but DC trademark on Joker name and likeness is real
Less popular in 3D printing = lower enforcement priority; trademark still applies
Autobot/Decepticon logos are globally trademarked; Optimus Prime face is a frequent DMCA target
Hasbro enforces aggressively especially after G4 popularity; pony character shapes flagged
D&D logo and specific character names (Beholder, Mind Flayer) trademarked; generic fantasy dice and dragons are low risk
Specific WotC monster design; generic multi-eyed monster is a grey area
WotC IP; same grey area as Beholder; avoid exact design reproduction
Barbie name and doll proportions trademarked; Mattel enforces Etsy listings
Logo and track designs trademarked; enforcement less common in 3D print space but risk exists
Creeper face is one of the most recognised and enforced gaming trademarks globally
The Minecraft creeper face design is trademarked; Microsoft has ramped enforcement since acquiring Mojang
Small indie studio; less aggressive than AAA publishers but IP is still protected; the round helmet shape is distinctive
Epic actively enforces; skins and character designs are proprietary
Roblox character head shape is trademarked; enforcement has increased as Roblox pursued brand partnerships
Character designs and logo enforced; Tracer and D.Va especially popular targets for 3D printing
Faction symbols (Horde/Alliance) trademarked; less frequent enforcement than Overwatch
Logo and character designs protected; less enforcement activity in 3D print community
Riot has issued DMCA takedowns on STL repositories; less Etsy activity so far
Newer IP; Riot enforcement growing; agent designs and logo both protected
Character designs and logo protected; enforcement less aggressive than Nintendo or Disney
Ghost design and Traveller sphere are recognisable IP; Bungie has been protective of brand
Logo and specific weapon/operator designs protected; enforcement in 3D print space is low but growing
Master Chief helmet is one of the most DMCA'd gaming 3D models globally; Microsoft enforces strongly
AI character from Halo; enforced alongside Halo IP
Kratos design and Leviathan Axe protected; Sony enforcement in 3D print space is moderate
Character likenesses and Clicker design protected; HBO series increased brand sensitivity
Machine designs are protected; relatively low enforcement in 3D print space
Disney acquired Fox in 2019; Simpsons fully enforced; Homer sponge holder is a known DMCA target
Same enforcement as Simpsons post-Disney acquisition; Bender design frequently targeted
Character designs enforced; Stewie and Peter most commonly flagged
Extremely popular in 3D printing; Adult Swim enforces actively with WB backing
Netflix has become significantly more aggressive in IP enforcement since 2022
Dalgona molds and guard masks are direct IP; Netflix issued takedowns globally
Shared IP creates complex enforcement; Geralt medallion is a common 3D print subject and has been flagged
Original character is older IP; Netflix series has renewed enforcement interest
Universal enforces; dinosaur silhouettes are protected when combined with JP branding; standalone dinosaurs without branding are generally safe
Character shapes are globally trademarked; Illumination enforces aggressively
Full IP enforced by Illumination Entertainment
All characters enforced; Woody, Buzz and Rex are common 3D print DMCA targets
Disney acquired Fox including the Alien IP; Xenomorph head is a frequent DMCA target
Same Disney/Fox enforcement as Alien; Predator mask is a frequent target
IP ownership is complex (multiple studio handoffs); T-800 skull is popular but enforcement is inconsistent
Manga/anime IP enforced in the West by Viz Media; headband and Sharingan eye are common 3D print DMCA targets
Toei Animation is one of the most aggressive anime IP enforcers globally
Toei enforces; specific Jolly Roger skull designs and character likenesses flagged
Less enforcement than DBZ/One Piece but IP is protected; survey corps wings design flagged
Growing enforcement as IP value increases; character costume designs protected
Tanjiro's earring design is trademarked and flagged on Etsy
Currently low enforcement activity in 3D print space; IP is protected but limited takedown history
Popular 3D print subject; miHoYo enforces in China and internationally
LEGO brick stud pattern is a registered trademark in many countries; brick-compatible designs have been disputed; LEGO has DMCA'd STL repos including Thingiverse
Funko vinyl figure proportions (large head, small body) are registered trade dress; Funko has DMCA'd STL repos for unauthorised character figures in Pop style
Round plush character shapes are protected; brand has grown enforcement activity as Squishmallow became a major collectible
Australian children's character; BBC enforces globally; Bluey and Bingo character shapes flagged on Etsy
Hasbro acquired eOne; Peppa character shape is globally enforced
Sanrio enforces aggressively globally; face and bow combination is globally trademarked; one of the most litigated character designs worldwide
Sanrio character; enforced with same aggression as Hello Kitty
Sanrio character; full enforcement
Sanrio character; full enforcement; popular in 3D print space
San-X enforces in Japan and increasingly internationally
Web comic character with active trademark enforcement; cat ghost shape is distinctive IP
Apple logo and product names trademarked; functional accessories (MagSafe stands, AirPod holders) generally tolerated WITHOUT the Apple logo or 'Designed for Apple' language
Tesla name and logo trademarked; functional car accessories tolerated but avoid Tesla wordmark or Supercharger logo in title
Exact-fit accessories generally tolerated; Dyson does not actively pursue third-party accessory makers; avoid implying official endorsement
Functional accessories tolerated; do not imply official affiliation; Whirlpool has not historically targeted 3D print accessory sellers
Ford name and logos trademarked; exact-fit parts without Ford branding generally tolerated; avoid Blue Oval or F-150 logo in photos
Jeep grille design is a registered trademark; round headlight designs disputed; functional accessories without Jeep wordmark are lower risk
